real provider reliability measured
candidate portfolio proves public novelty
public release approved
clone readiness replaces hosted publication review

claim_boundary: Fixture-level proof of release-candidate grammar and repair routing; not a public release, private-root equivalence claim, or novelty proof.

grammar_rules.blocks: Candidate rows that do not name how the release version improves the source surface.
grammar_rules.repair_contract: Write a concrete improvement_delta before any card or demo can reuse the row.

grammar_rules.blocks: Candidate rows without provenance or source anchors.
grammar_rules.repair_contract: Add public-safe source, standard, fixture, or receipt refs before ranking the row.

grammar_rules.blocks: Projection strategies outside the declared release vocabulary.
grammar_rules.repair_contract: Choose one allowed projection mode and keep copy-then-sanitize out of the primitive.

grammar_rules.blocks: Rows that substitute vague praise for a mechanism, evidence boundary, or repair action.
grammar_rules.repair_contract: Replace praise with the mechanism, the proof surface, and the next executable action.

grammar_rules.blocks: Rows that instruct publication before disclosure, license, citation, and probe gates are current.
grammar_rules.repair_contract: Route to a fail-closed gate or probe refresh instead of a public claim.

improvement_delta: The release specimen turns candidate standards and provider canary status channels into deterministic grammar-to-worker-action transaction rows.

provider_replay_bridge_contract.claim_boundary: Provider replay rows are synthetic fixture routing only; they do not measure real provider reliability or grant hosted-public status.

public_safety_boundary: Synthetic candidate mutations only; no private root, raw operator voice, private registry, or private standards engine is included.
publication_boundary: Website cards and external claims stay blocked until registry, disclosure, license, citation, clean-run, clone, and hosted gates are current.

anti_claims: local executable grammar capsules only
not private standards authority
not real provider reliability evidence
not public release approval

carryforward_contract.rule: Every grammar or provider replay decision that becomes a reusable route must carry its source clip, deterministic hash, evaluator authority, worker action, transaction status, and anti-claims.

anti_claims: grammar capsule is not private standards engine authority
grammar capsule is not public release approval
grammar capsule is not novelty proof
grammar_case_capsules.authority_boundary: A grammar capsule proves deterministic fixture carryforward only; it is not public release approval, private standards authority, or novelty evidence.
grammar_case_capsules.omission_boundary: Capsule contains only public-safe release-candidate fixture mutation data and grammar repair routing; no private registry, raw operator voice, or private standards engine is included.

semantic_carryforward.carryforward_rule: Carry the grammar decision with the exact mutated source clip, rule ids, worker action, transaction status, and anti-claim boundary before treating the rule as reusable.
